title: Employee Participation During the Centenary
primary_topics: Valentine's Day appreciation of court employees
sub_topics: Sexual Dysfunction Among Lay People seminar (December 7, 2000) — with Pfizer
Mass housing program with PAG-IBIG (Justice Pardo sub-committee)
Search for Special Centennial Awardees for Excellence (Bellosillo and Feria)
Dumaguete grand debate (February 9, 2001, Ateneo vs Silliman)

stances.claim: The centenary celebration on Valentine's Day for SC, CA, and DOJ employees deliberately emphasizes 'na ang mga pinakamalapit sa puso ng Supreme Court ay ang mga kawaning naglilingkod sa hustisya' — those closest to the heart of the SC are the employees serving justice.

Without our employees, we cannot speak of the judiciary and the justice system — 'an office, however exalted or however lowly, can be effective only when it has the support and the cooperation of the people composing it.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-importance framing of judicial workforce + aphoristic universal-organizational principle

stances.claim: The most important components of judicial dispensation are 'the men and women who compose the judicial branch of government' — not the magistrates or the case docket, but the workforce.

signature_phrases: Magandang hapon po sa inyong lahat
ang mga pinakamalapit sa puso ng Supreme Court ay ang mga kawaning naglilingkod sa hustisya
an office, however exalted or however lowly, can be effective only when it has the support and the cooperation of the people composing it
the men and women who compose the judicial branch of government
register_markers: extended Tagalog opening (bilingual SC speech tradition)
Tagalog code-switching throughout (Magandang hapon, Maligayang Araw ng mga Puso, wala pong palalabasin)
people-first closer (men and women who compose the judicial branch)
speech register addressing employees rather than peers
decision_framework_signals: applies people-first framing for institutional accountability (workforce as primary stakeholder)
uses universal-organizational-effectiveness principle (high-or-low office, same employee-dependence)
frames employee-welfare programs (sports, health seminars, housing, loyalty awards) as institutional-care infrastructure
notable_anecdotes: Dumaguete debate having more than 800 attendees each paying P50 admission for a 2.5-hour debate (8:00-10:30 pm)
Region 7 Cebu City employees holding a two-day centenary celebration
